title: Action: Align OEM lead windows + cloud backstop for Acme Financial Dedicated Pool
preview:
From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
To: Wei Liu <wei.liu@oemgpu.com>, Alex Turner <alex.turner@strata-cloud.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@acmefinancial.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 11 Aug 2026 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Align OEM lead windows + cloud backstop for Acme Financial Dedicated Pool

Hi Wei / Alex / Acme procurement team,

Thanks for taking this on. Quick context: Acme Financial (deal ACME-4127) has accepted the term economics in principle and needs a dedicated GPU pool sized for their peak analytic workloads. They want a December onramp window but are open to a staged delivery if we can't fully populate the pool by then. High-level ask: we need confirmed OEM lead times + a cloud backfill plan so we can finalize the delivery milestones and the PO.

Sizing note (initial ask from Acme): 200 inference-optimized GPUs (roughly equivalent to 200 A100/80GB-class devices in total FLOPS). Acme's preferred delivery cadence: up to 120 units available by 2026-12-01, remainder by 2027-01-31. If that is not feasible, they will accept a hybrid delivery with cloud-provisioned burst capacity until hardware arrives.

Specific asks:
- Wei: can you confirm SKU-level ETAs, earliest ship week, and deposit/payment cadence? Please attach a formal quote.
- Alex: please confirm whether Strata can guarantee a regional backstop (us-west) for the 200-GPU peak between Dec 1 and Feb 1, and provide cost delta for 50%/100% burst scenarios.
- Acme procurement: please confirm shipping address and whether they require onsite setup (racking/cabling) versus delivered-to-data-center.

I've attached the Acme hardware requirements sheet (acme_hw_requirements.xlsx) with the target performance envelope and redundant power/thermal constraints.

Goal: finalize milestones and PO terms this week so we can lock lead windows with OEM and present a single timeline to Acme CS.

Thanks,
Chloe Martin
Director, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: acme_hw_requirements.xlsx (spreadsheet, 12 KB)

From: Wei Liu <wei.liu@oemgpu.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Alex Turner <alex.turner@strata-cloud.com>
Cc: Sales Ops <sales.ops@oemgpu.com>
Date: Tue, 11 Aug 2026 11:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Align OEM lead windows + cloud backstop for Acme Financial Dedicated Pool

Hi Chloe / Ben / Alex,

Thanks for the details and the requirements workbook — helpful. Short answers:

- Lead time: current manufacturing queue for the quoted SKU is 18-22 weeks from PO confirmation and deposit. That puts earliest ship weeks in late-November to mid-December depending on slot pickup.
- Minimum commit and deposits: OEM requires a 50% deposit on the hardware portion at PO; balance due on ship.
- Quote: attached (oem_quote_202608.pdf). The quote includes a two-tranche shipping option (Tier A: up to 120 units shipped as available; Tier B: backfill for remaining units). Pricing is tiered based on total quantity and is valid for 14 days.
- Logistics: we can ship to your data-center or to our integrator partner for racking+test before handoff. Lead time variance (+/- 2 weeks) is driven by component sourcing (PSU inventory and backplane pickups).

Questions for Redwood:
1) Can Acme accept a staged acceptance with partial SLA until full pool is provisioned?
2) Please confirm final SKU part numbers and rack density (units/rack) so we can finalize transport packaging and hazardous materials compliance.

I attached the quote and our standard ship schedule template. Happy to hop on a quick call tomorrow to walk through details.

Best,
Wei Liu
Enterprise Accounts 
OEM GPU Systems

--
Attachment: oem_quote_202608.pdf (PDF, 95 KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 11 Aug 2026 12:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Align OEM lead windows + cloud backstop for Acme Financial Dedicated Pool

Chloe / team — quick internal alignment before we reply to OEM/Strata.

Summary of options I see:
- Option A (Staggered OEM delivery): Commit to 120 units now (earliest ship late-Nov) and tranche remaining 80 in Jan. Requires Acme sign-off on staged SLA and 50% deposit.
- Option B (Cloud backstop): Ask Strata to provide a reserved backstop for up to 200 GPUs (burstable) from 2026-12-01 to 2027-02-01. Actual charge would be higher per-hour but avoids assembly lead-time risk.
- Option C (Hybrid acceptance + temporary reserved pool): Mix: 120 OEM + 80 Strata burst until OEM completes.

Action items / asks:
- Karthik: can the Dedicated product support a soft-start where capacity is partially provisioned and routing/pinning is added as hardware arrives? We need rollover behavior documented for SLOs.
- Vanessa: can you push for a 30% deposit cap on Acme (finance prefers lower upfront), or negotiate a delivery milestone payment schedule?

I think Option C is the most defensible commercially — keeps Acme covered and reduces cash exposure if we structure milestone-based payments. I can draft the mutual action plan once Karthik confirms the soft-start behavior and Alex confirms firm burst capacity numbers.

Ben
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

On Tue, Aug 11, 2026 at 11:15 AM Wei Liu <wei.liu@oemgpu.com> wrote:
> Hi Chloe / Ben / Alex,
> 
> Thanks for the details and the requirements workbook — helpful. Short answers:
> 
> - Lead time: current manufacturing queue for the quoted SKU is 18-22 weeks from PO confirmation and deposit. That puts earliest ship weeks in late-November to mid-December depending on slot pickup.
> - Minimum commit and deposits: OEM requires a 50% deposit on the hardware portion at PO; balance due on ship.
> - Quote: attached (oem_quote_202608.pdf). The quote includes a two-tranche shipping option (Tier A: up to 120 units shipped as available; Tier B: backfill for remaining units). Pricing is tiered based on total quantity and is valid for 14 days.

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
To: Wei Liu <wei.liu@oemgpu.com>, Alex Turner <alex.turner@strata-cloud.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Procurement Team <procurement@acmefinancial.com>
Date: Wed, 12 Aug 2026 16:34:00 -07:00
Subject: Next steps: PO cadence, soft-start mechanics, and scheduling call (Acme / OEM / Strata)

Team / Wei / Alex / Acme procurement,

Thanks — good progress. Quick consolidated next steps so we have an agreed single source of truth:

1) Commercials & PO cadence
- Acme to confirm whether they accept staged acceptance with partial SLA (Ben: please take point with Acme and propose milestone acceptance language).
- Vanessa to negotiate deposit from 50% -> 30% or construct 3-way milestone payment (30% on PO, 40% on first ship, 30% on final acceptance).
- Acme procurement: please confirm billing address and PO approver name.

2) Soft-start / Dedicated behavior (Karthik action)
- Karthik to confirm soft-start semantics for Dedicated pools (expected behavior: routes can be pinned to the arriving tranche; SLOs prorated for partially provisioned pool; cache priming window expected). Please provide a short doc (1 page) by EOD tomorrow so we can include in the mutual action plan.

3) Cloud backstop (Alex)
- Alex to provide a firm on-paper committed backstop quote for us-west region for two scenarios: 50% burst and 100% burst (Dec 1–Feb 1). Include performance equivalence assumptions and activation lead time.

4) Logistics / SKU confirmation (Wei)
- Wei to confirm final part numbers for all 200 units and confirm whether OEM will handle integrator racking + burn-in if we want units ship-ready. Also confirm shipping weeks and any test-scheduling constraints.

5) Call to align
- Proposed times: Thu Aug 13 at 8:00am PT or Thu Aug 13 at 3:00pm PT. Please reply with availability; I'll send a calendar invite and a short agenda.

6) Attachments & links
- I'm re-attaching acme_hw_requirements.xlsx for reference and including the OEM quote. I've started a HubSpot milestone (link above) and will add meeting notes.

If we get the soft-start doc and Strata's backstop numbers by EOD tomorrow, I can finalize the mutual action plan and draft a PO template for Acme this week.

Appreciate everyone's partnership here.

-Chloe
Director, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

--
Forwarded message:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 11 Aug 2026 12:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Align OEM lead windows + cloud backstop for Acme Financial Dedicated Pool
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>

(Internal note: Ben suggested Option C — staging + cloud backstop.)

--

title: Founder bundle credit allocation & ops path
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Neal <liam@launchlabs.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 10 Aug 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Founder bundle credit allocation & ops path

Hi Liam — great to meet last week. Per our call, attaching the Founder Bundle term sheet and our current pricing sheet so your ops and finance folks can see the mechanics. Quick summary of what LaunchLabs asked for and what we're proposing: 

- Ask: split $50k in promotional credits across sandbox/onboarding (for mentor teams + demos) and production pilot usage; want ability to convert leftover credits to a Dedicated commitment later.
- Proposal (recommended): 60% sandbox/onboarding (to maximize demo velocity) + 40% pilot/production; credits usable against hosted API and convertible to a prorated Dedicated credit within 9 months; credits expire 12 months from activation.

Two commercial options we can offer: 
1) Standard allocation (no additional discount) — credits applied to usage until exhaustion.
2) Bundled onboarding (recommended for LaunchLabs): 50/50 split, + 10% applied discount on the first 3 months of paid usage after credits are exhausted, and a conversion credit to Dedicated if they convert within 9 months (conversion formula: remaining credit value * 0.85).

Operational asks for your team: can contracts@launchlabs.io accept the conversion language into the addendum (we can produce a short DocuSign-ready addendum), and do you have a preferred invoice path (po number or marketplace)?

I've attached a redlined term sheet (LaunchLabs_FounderBundle_v2.pdf) and our pricing worksheet. If you want, we can do a 20-min ops call with your finance on Thursday.

Next steps: confirm preferred split and whether conversion-to-Dedicated is acceptable; I’ll loop in legal for an addendum draft.

Thanks —
Ava
Ava Chen
Growth Sales
Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0198

Attachments: LaunchLabs_FounderBundle_v2.pdf (term sheet, 3 pages), redwood-pricing-sheet-2026.xlsx (pricing workbook)


From: Liam O'Neal <liam@launchlabs.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: contracts@launchlabs.io
Date: Tue, 11 Aug 2026 09:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Founder bundle credit allocation & ops path

Thanks, Ava — appreciate the clear options and the docs. A few quick clarifying Qs from our side before we pull finance/legal in: 

1) Private/on-prem: can any portion of the credits be applied to a Private deployment (we may need to run a small on-prem pilot for our regulated cohort)?
2) Expiry: you mentioned 12 months from activation — is there flexibility to extend to 18 months for strategic partners that have longer procurement cycles?
3) Invoice path: we prefer PO + standard net-30 invoice, but our marketplace procurement team (cc’d) also asked whether marketplace billing (AWS Marketplace) is possible for credits conversion.

If the conversion-to-Dedicated formula can be circulated as a short addendum, contracts can review quickly. Happy to take the 20-min ops call — Thursday at 10am ET works for us.

Liam
Liam O'Neal
Partnerships Lead, LaunchLabs
liam@launchlabs.io


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 12 Aug 2026 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Founder bundle credit allocation & ops path

Ben / Marissa — forwarding LaunchLabs’ follow-up so we can lock commercial and ops. Key asks: private/on-prem applicability, expiry flexibility to 18 months, and invoice path PO vs marketplace. My recommendations below.

Recommendation (commercial + ops):
- Allocation: go with the Bundled onboarding offer but tweak to 55% sandbox / 45% pilot. Rationale: preserves demo velocity while giving LaunchLabs confidence they can run meaningful pilots.
- Private deployments: allow up to $10k of the credit pool to be earmarked for Private (on-prem/VPC) pilot usage. This must be explicitly tracked in billing and flagged on invoices.
- Expiry: extend to 15 months (compromise) for strategic partners; if they need 18 months, require an executive sponsor sign-off and log it on the deal.
- Conversion to Dedicated: accept the conversion clause as 85% of remaining credit value (our proposed formula). Add a clause that conversion must be invoked within 9 months of activation.
- Discount sweetener: keep the 10% applied discount for first 3 months post-credits.
- Invoice path: support PO + net-30 as default. Marketplace billing only if customer wants marketplace credits converted to Redwood invoicing (ops overhead and delays).

Ops asks: Marissa — can you confirm we can carve $10k of credits for Private pilots and flag those on the invoice? Ben — if you agree, I’ll instruct legal to draft a 1-page addendum and populate the DocuSign template (link below). Also propose we use the HubSpot deal DL-2026-043 for tracking.

If everyone agrees, I’ll send the addendum to contracts@launchlabs.io and schedule the 20-min ops call for Thursday 10am ET.

DocuSign addendum draft (placeholder): https://na2.docusign.net/Member/PowerFormSigning.aspx?PowerFormId=abc-123
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/deal/DEAL-2026-043

Thanks —
Ava

To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: contracts@launchlabs.io
Date: Tue, 11 Aug 2026 09:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Founder bundle credit allocation & ops path

(see above)

title: MAP: TidalWorks vendor enrollment & procurement checkpoints
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Turner <alex.turner@tidalworks.com>
Cc: 
Date: Mon, 10 Aug 2026 15:42:00 -0700
Subject: MAP draft + vendor onboarding checkpoints (post-call recap)

Alex — great talking today, thanks for the time. Quick recap from our discovery and an initial MAP (attached) that focuses on the vendor/procurement milestones we discussed. I've kept the onboarding items minimal so they're easy to track and assign — below are the suggested checkpoints, owners, and dependency notes.

Attachments: TidalWorks_MAP_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet with milestones)
Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cTidalWorksMAP

Suggested MAP milestones (draft):
1) Vendor registration (Ariba/Coupa)
   - Owner: TidalWorks procurement
   - Target: 2026-08-24
   - Dependencies: Ariba account + supplier profile info

2) Vendor forms (W-9, standard vendor questionnaire, banking details)
   - Owner: TidalWorks (submit), Redwood (provide templates / pre-fill where applicable)
   - Target: 2026-08-18
   - Dependencies: Finance contact at TidalWorks; completed banking form

3) Redwood validation & PO terms confirmation (pricing + invoicing cadence)
   - Owner: Redwood Finance / Legal (Priyom will own validations)
   - Target: 2026-08-26
   - Dependencies: Completed vendor forms, any contract redlines

4) First invoice test / payment verification
   - Owner: Redwood Finance + TidalWorks AP
   - Target: 2026-09-01

Target close for onboarding (ready to bill): 2026-09-07 (tentative)

Open items / asks for you: please confirm the internal owners (procurement contact + AP), whether you're using Ariba or Coupa (I think you mentioned Ariba on the call), and whether the target dates above look feasible. If you'd like, we can schedule a 20-minute working session next week (proposed: Tue 8/17) to walk the MAP with your procurement lead and mine.

Notes for Redwood team (internal): I'll loop in Priyom for vendor validation and Lauren for SLA/ops review once procurement confirms owners.

Thanks — attaching the MAP V1 for quick edits.

—
Ben Carter
Senior Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | (415) 555-0182

- Tentative dates from our side: we can aim to submit the vendor forms (W-9 + banking) by 2026-08-17 and to have Ariba registration completed by 2026-08-25. That aligns closely with your targets.

—
Alex Turner

title: Credential demarcation & tenant gate: initial orchestration
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@cypress-analytics.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 07 Aug 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Credential demarcation & tenant gate: initial orchestration

Hi Elena —

Congrats again on signing. To make the initial tenant launch smooth, here’s a concise proposed plan for credentialing, roles, and API key seeding. We want to minimize permission friction while meeting your security team's KMS requirement.

High level (week 0 -> week 2):
- Day 0: Kickoff call (30m) to confirm owner list and KMS scope.
- Day 1–2: Redwood creates the tenant namespace and seeds a read-only service account for telemetry verification.
- Day 3: Customer security receives KMS integration notes for wrapping keys and trust boundary.
- Day 4–7: Seed API keys in a staging project; customer validates with a smoke test (token + sample prompt).
- Week 2: Promote seeded keys to production project and invite admin users; rotate ephemeral keys.

Questions / asks for your security team before kickoff:
1) Does your SOC prefer Redwood-managed envelope keys or an external KMS binding?
2) List of initial admin users (email + role).
3) Any IP egress restrictions for staging that we should configure ahead of time.

Attached: tenant_onboard_checklist.pdf — a short checklist we’ll run during the kickoff.

Proposed kickoff slots (PDT): Mon 10:00–11:00, Tue 13:30–14:00, Wed 09:00–09:30. Pick one and we’ll send a calendar invite.

Thanks — I’ll coordinate internal tasks once we lock a time.

—
Ben Carter
Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference


From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@cypress-analytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: security@cypress-analytics.com, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 07 Aug 2026 12:04:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Credential demarcation & tenant gate: initial orchestration

Ben — thanks for the clear plan. A few notes from our side (cc'ing security):

- We require customer-managed KMS (AWS KMS) for production tenant keys. Staging can use Redwood-managed keys for speed, but production must be bound to our KMS key alias.
- Initial admin list: elena.morales@cypress-analytics.com (Org Admin), samir.kapoor@cypress-analytics.com (SRE).
- Egress: staging must only talk to your staging endpoints and our IP range 35.201.0.0/16. Production will have broader rules later.

We’re happy to do Tue 13:30–14:00 PDT for kickoff.

On the KMS binding: Do you have a runbook or minimum IAM policy you prefer we grant for key-wrap operations? If you can share that, our infra team will prepare the customer side.

Thanks — looking forward to the kickoff.

Elena
Head of Platform
Cypress Analytics

On Fri, Aug 7, 2026 at 09:18 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (quoted original plan)


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elena Morales <elena.morales@cypress-analytics.com>
Date: Mon, 10 Aug 2026 08:15:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Credential demarcation & tenant gate: initial orchestration

Looping internal owners and spelling out immediate action items before Tuesday’s kickoff.

Ben / Jonas — per Elena: customer-managed AWS KMS in production, staging can be Redwood-managed. They supplied two initial admins and an IP range for staging. Kickoff is confirmed for Tue 13:30 PDT.

Internal checklist (I’ll own coordination):
- [Ben] Prepare tenant namespace and attach staging project (deadline: Tue 11:00 PDT). Attach: api_key_seeding_script.sh for Jonas to run during the smoke test.
- [Jonas] Prepare the KMS integration notes and minimal IAM policy. I attached kms_integration_notes.pdf for Elena; please validate and expand with the exact policy ARN we recommend.
- [Monica] Create internal runbook page and update the HubSpot deal with the kickoff outcome.

We should also prepare a short smoke-test prompt and a sample telemetry dashboard so Cypress can confirm tokens are scoped correctly.

Attachments: kms_integration_notes.pdf, api_key_seeding_script.sh

Monica Patel
Implementation Lead, CSM Operations
Redwood Inference

On Fri, Aug 7, 2026 at 12:04 PM Elena Morales <elena.morales@cypress-analytics.com> wrote:
> (their confirmation)


From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Elena Morales <elena.morales@cypress-analytics.com>
Date: Wed, 12 Aug 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Credential demarcation & tenant gate: initial orchestration

Forwarding the IAM snippet and the minimal KMS policy we’ll ask Cypress to attach. I also added two scheduling notes at the bottom — one is a DocuSign link for the order form (they signed, but we need the final prod KMS addendum).

Policy snippet (inline):
- kms:Encrypt/kms:Decrypt restricted to key alias arn:aws:kms:us-west-2:123456789012:alias/cypress-prod (example)
- kms:GenerateDataKeyWithContext for envelope keys

Next steps post-kickoff:
1) Run seed script in staging (Jonas) — run window Tue 14:15–14:30 PDT
2) Cypress runs smoke test and returns sample logs (Elena) — target Tue 15:00 PDT
3) If green, we schedule prod KMS binding and orderform sign (DocuSign). HubSpot link below for deal notes.

HubSpot: https://hubspot.redwood.com/deals/CYP-4521
DocuSign (order addendum): https://docsend.com/d/tenant-setup-orderform

Thanks — will be online for the Tue window and can join the kickoff to run the seed.

—Jonas
Platform Engineer
Redwood Inference

title: Post-sign handoff: Cedar Health — implementation owner + assets
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.inference>, Lina Park <lina@cedarhealth.com>, Evan Rhee <evan.rhee@cedarhealth.com>
Date: 2026-08-09 09:12 -0700
Subject: Post-sign handoff: Cedar Health — implementation owner + assets

Team —

Great to loop you in. Cedar (Lina/Evan) signed the SOW last night (deal id RWD-2026-CH-0843). Key context up front:
- Initial footprint: 4 dedicated inference instances (g5x2) in us-west with burst capacity; expected steady-state: ~15k tokens/min peak, batchy traffic daytime PST.
- Data residency: US-only, logs retained 90 days.
- Integration: server-to-server API (JWT), connector for their internal triage tool; they will push patient-metadata to a protected queue.
- Go-live target: 2026-09-20 (hard date from Cedar execs).
- Important constraints: SOC2 evidence required for audit by 2026-09-05; they will need a network cut (IP allowlist + peering) planned in the week prior to go-live.

Attachments: signed_SOW_Cedar_2026_08.pdf (signed copy), network_requirements_cedar.xlsx (ports/IPs), cedar_sample_requests.csv (sample events for smoke tests).

Action items I need from CS (Monica/Karthik):
1) Nominate an implementation owner and confirm capacity to hit 9/20 go-live.
2) Propose a 3-week runbook (kickoff, staging, smoke, go-live) with dates and owners.
3) Confirm which Redwood checkpoints (security evidence, test dataset ingestion) you want me to own vs. hand off.

Lina/Evan — Monica and Karthik are the CS/implementation leads from our side. Monica will be primary success lead; we propose Karthik to run the technical rollout if that works for you.

Ben
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

---
Reply-to thread below for visibility.


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.inference>, Lina Park <lina@cedarhealth.com>, Evan Rhee <evan.rhee@cedarhealth.com>
Date: 2026-08-09 11:02 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-sign handoff: Cedar Health — implementation owner + assets

Thanks Ben — congrats on the close. Quick acknowledgement and a few clarifying asks so we can scope the runbook:

- We can support the 9/20 go-live but will need a firm staging window the week before (9/13–9/17) for performance tuning and peering tests.
- Lina/Evan: can you confirm the list of admin contacts who will have access to your account (name, email, role)? We need at least one network engineer for IP verification during peering work.
- Can you confirm whether Cedar wants session-level user tracing in logs? That affects retention and compliance scope.

Initial CS asks from our side:
- Provide a sample of production-ish traffic (the cedar_sample_requests.csv you attached) and a preferred dataset for our functional smoke tests.
- Please add the Redwood onboarding contact (onboarding@cedarhealth.com) to any external tickets related to networking.

Ben — please nominate who will be the single point to hand security evidence to (SOC2 artifacts). If we can get the signed SOW and network spreadsheet in the shared drive link, we’ll start a lightweight runbook draft by EOD 8/10.

preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sana Gupta <sana.gupta@medbridge.ai>, Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@medbridge.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 11 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Invitation: joint technical webinar — Serving LLMs at scale (customer tech talk)

Hi Sana & Tom,

Hope you’re both well. We’d love to feature MedBridge in a short technical customer session we’re running for platform and infrastructure engineers — the working title is “Serving LLMs at scale: lessons from MedBridge.”

Format proposal: 30-minute customer tech talk (your engineering lead + our runtime engineer), followed by 15 minutes of live Q&A. Audience will be platform/ML infra engineers from ~30 companies (targeted).

We’d handle slides and logistics, and will work with you for any content you’d prefer to keep internal. I attached a very rough draft agenda for timing. A few logistics questions to start:
- Any dates in early–mid September that work for your team? (we’re looking at Sept 8, 15, or 22)
- Do you need an internal review cycle for slides and talking points?
- Any security/compliance constraints for live demos or screenshots?

If you’re up for it, we can follow with a short one-pager speaker release and a quick run-of-show.

Attachments: medbridge-tech-talk-draft-agenda.pdf (PDF, 1 page)

Thanks — looking forward to it.

Best,
Ben Carter

ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0123

title: Recap: Discovery — Silverline Pay pilot & cost levers
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morris <elena@silverline-pay.com>, Raj Patel <raj.patel@silverline-pay.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 09 Aug 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Recap: Discovery — Silverline Pay pilot & cost levers

Elena / Raj — thanks again for taking time today. Quick recap from our discovery + clear next steps so we’re aligned.

What we heard (pain / goals)
- Volume profile: ~200–350M tokens/month in Q1 (peak in fraud review flows).
- Latency SLA: 250–400ms p95 for user-facing scoring; more tolerant for async reconciliation.
- Budget posture: constrained pilot budget (~$50–80k/month cap) — procurement needs a clear ROI and predictable TCO.
- Data controls: PII redaction + audit logs required; likely Private or Dedicated if we move past pilot.

Primary success criteria for pilot (agreed)
- Show 30–40% unit-cost savings vs their current self-hosted CPU baseline via batching + KV cache.
- Maintain model quality (no regressive generation) on our eval suite for fraud intents.
- Demonstrate predictable monthly spend within the stated budget window or surface tradeoffs if we need higher throughput.

Cost/TCO levers we discussed (what we’ll quantify in the proposal)
1) Batching: continuous small-window batching for async flows — 20–35% token-cost reduction depending on concurrency.
2) KV/prefix caching: high reuse in decision logs gives big wins on chat-like flows (expected 15–25% savings at 40% cache hit).
3) Quantized lighter variants: 8-bit/4-bit model variants for background scoring (lower cost, slightly higher latency).
4) Routing policy: route non-critical traffic to cheaper regions/model variants.

Immediate next steps (owner / ETA)
- I (Ben) will follow up with a 90-day pilot TCO snapshot (ballpark + assumptions) and a short pilot plan doc: what we’ll instrument, SLAs, and a rollback path. (By Wed 11 Aug)
- Elena to confirm target pilot start date and procurement constraints (budget holder + approval lead). (Elena)
- Marissa to draft pricing options / reserved capacity scenarios if they need Dedicated. (Marissa)

Attachments: I’m including the assumptions workbook and a compact pilot worksheet (attached). I’ll flag the rows with the most sensitive inputs so procurement can see where the knobs are.

Thanks — will send the numbers Wednesday.

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter | Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0187

Attachment stubs: SLP_pilot_proposal.xlsx (workbook) — token assumptions + timeline; token_model_assumptions.pdf (one-pager)


From: Elena Morris <elena@silverline-pay.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Raj Patel <raj.patel@silverline-pay.com>, Tom Chen <tom.chen@silverline-pay.com>
Date: Mon, 09 Aug 2026 17:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Recap: Discovery — Silverline Pay pilot & cost levers

Thanks Ben — good summary and aligned on the levers. A couple clarifications from procurement side:
- The $50–80k/month is our absolute soft cap for a pilot (we'd prefer <$60k if possible).
- Critical ask: the proposal needs a 3-line summary that an FP&A partner can read quickly (ops impact, expected monthly cost, estimated ROI timeframe).
- Success measurement: we need a net-dollar ROI estimate tied to reduced manual review hours (Tom) and chargeback reduction (Raj).

If you can include a conservative and optimistic case that maps to the levers you listed, that will help fast-track approvals.

Also: we’d like to start the pilot the week of Sep 6 if timelines work — procurement needs a calendar target.

Elena
Elena Morris | Head of Payments Operations, Silverline Pay
elena@silverline-pay.com | +1 (212) 555-1299


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morris <elena@silverline-pay.com>, Raj Patel <raj.patel@silverline-pay.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 11 Aug 2026 08:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Recap: Discovery — Silverline Pay pilot & cost levers

Elena — thanks, attached is the promised 90-day snapshot + a two-row exec summary up front to hand to FP&A. Key highlights (conservative / optimistic):

Exec summary (two lines)
- Conservative: Pilot ~ $62k/mo; expected payback vs current manual/CPU baseline in ~5–7 months from reduced manual reviews and chargeback avoidance.
- Optimistic: Pilot ~ $44k/mo with aggressive batching + cache + partial quantization; payback in ~3–4 months.

Assumptions called out in the workbook (see "Key Inputs" sheet)
- Monthly tokens: base 220M / high 340M (distribution across scoring vs async reconciliation).
- Average tokens per request: 120 tokens (score + context).
- Cache hit range: 10–45% (we show 20% and 40% scenarios).
- Model variant mix: full-fp model for user-path; quantized variant for background.

Where the biggest delta comes from
- Batching for reconciliation windows (small latency acceptable) — 20–35% savings on those tokens.
- KV cache — if reuse is 40% we show ~18% savings overall.
- If we need Dedicated reserved capacity the unit economics improve but there is a monthly commit required (I outline break-even volumes in the workbook).

Quick asks from you to finalize pricing:
1) Confirm target pilot volume range (low/nominal/high).
2) Confirm pilot start week (you mentioned Sep 6).
3) Any procurement paperwork we should prepare in advance (NDA/PO templates).

I’ve attached "SLP_pilot_proposal.xlsx" with the conservative/optimistic scenarios and a one-page "token_model_assumptions.pdf" that calls out the knobs. If helpful, we can do a short 20m call with Tom + Raj to walk FP&A through the two-line summary.

Forwarding to Marissa and Karthik internally to align pricing and reserved scenarios.

Best,
Ben

Attachment stubs: SLP_pilot_proposal.xlsx; token_model_assumptions.pdf


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 11 Aug 2026 09:06:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Recap: Discovery — Silverline Pay pilot & cost levers

Marissa / Karthik — forwarding the thread from Silverline Pay. They want a conservative + optimistic TCO that fits under ~ $60k/mo ideally, pilot target week Sep 6.

What I need from you:
- Marissa: please draft 2 pricing options: (A) Hosted pilot w/ usage pricing that matches the conservative/optimistic rows in the workbook; (B) Dedicated reserved capacity option with monthly commit and break-even calc. Include any standard pilot discount language.
- Karthik: can you run a quick Dedicated sizing calc and the reserved cost per month at 3-month vs 12-month commit assumptions? Add notes for where quality/regressions could drive variant mix.

We should aim to get the pricing deck back to Elena by EOD Thu 12 Aug so they can circulate to FP&A. If you want a quick 15m to align, I’m free at 1pm today.

Thanks,
Ben

title: SLA template: uptime guarantee and monthly remedy formula (Synapse)
preview:
From: Dave Allen <dave.allen@synapse.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Synapse Legal <legal@synapse.com>
Date: Fri, 7 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Draft SLA and questions on monthly remedy formula

Jordan —

Thanks for sending the initial template. Attached is Synapse's procurement markup and a short list of questions we need clarified before procurement can move to PO. High-level items:

1) Uptime: We're asking for a 99.95% monthly availability commitment for our production cluster (multi-AZ).
2) Remedy: Synapse prefers a pro-rata monthly credit tied to % downtime rather than a fixed cap; they want clear calculation examples.
3) Maintenance: we need a defined maintenance carve-out + a 72-hour advance notice requirement.
4) Regional failover: how will cross-region failovers count toward SLA?

I've attached Synapse_marked_up_SLA.pdf for reference. Can you confirm Redwood's standard stance and whether the remedy calculation below is negotiable?

Synapse proposed remedy (excerpt):
- If monthly availability >= 99.95%: no credit
- 99.90%–99.94%: 5% credit of monthly invoice
- 99.0%–99.89%: 15% credit
- <99.0%: 30% credit

Thanks — happy to hop on a quick call if needed.

Best,
Dave
Procurement Manager | Synapse Systems

Attachment: Synapse_marked_up_SLA.pdf (pdf)


From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 7 Aug 2026 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Draft SLA and questions on monthly remedy formula

Team — forwarding Synapse's markup and asks. They're pushing for 99.95% availability and a pro-rata credit ladder (see Dave's email). Key negotiation points I want input on:

- Can we accept 99.95% for multi-AZ production in their region (us-west-2) without a major price uplift?
- Remedy structure: their ladder is steeper than our standard. What's the nearest equivalent we can offer that preserves a cap but gives a clearer example table?
- Maintenance and regional failover carve-outs: sync with Product/Infra guidance so we're not over-promising.

I've attached our baseline SLA draft (SLA_Synapse_v1.docx) and created a redlined PDF with my initial comments. Please add quick edits and flag any non-starters. I'm aiming to get a consolidated position back to Dave early next week.

Thanks,
Jordan
Head of Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference

Attachment: SLA_Synapse_v1.docx (docx), SLA_Synapse_v1_redlines.pdf (pdf)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 10 Aug 2026 09:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Draft SLA and questions on monthly remedy formula

Jordan / team — my take after reading their ladder:

- 99.95% ask is doable for a committed, dedicated pool (reserved capacity) but not for hosted burst instances. We should tie 99.95% to a Dedicated contract or an uplifted rate. For hosted, 99.9% is our baseline.

- Remedy table: rather than full pro-rata, propose a hybrid: small automatic credit bands plus an annualized cap. Example:
  * >=99.95%: 0%
  * 99.90–99.949%: 3%
  * 99.00–99.899%: 10%
  * <99.00%: 25%
  Cap: monthly credits cannot exceed 50% of monthly invoice (we can propose an alternate cap if they push).

- Maintenance carve-out: we should keep a 48-hour advance notice for scheduled maintenance for service impacting events; for emergency maintenance, define rapid escalation and a postmortem within 7 business days. Synapse's 72-hour ask is fine if they're a Dedicated customer.

- Regional failover: failovers initiated by customer (e.g., their DNS changes) should not count toward Redwood's availability. Automated failover due to our regional outage should be covered.

I'll draft language for the remedy formula and the cap. Flag if you want different numbers before I join the call with Synapse.

-Ben
Sr. Commercial Manager | Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 10 Aug 2026 11:27:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Draft SLA and questions on monthly remedy formula

Ben — +1 on tying 99.95% to Dedicated. A few product/legal notes from my side after scanning the doc: 

1) We need to call out the quantization of maintenance windows (max 4 hours per maintenance event for minor upgrades) and a monthly aggregate (e.g., up to 8 hours/month scheduled maintenance that won't count as downtime).
2) For the credit calculation examples, include an explicit formula: monthly credit = (monthly invoice) * (% downtime bucket credit). Also add a worked example to avoid back-and-forth during procurement.
3) For multi-region failover: clarify that cross-region replication lag that causes degraded performance counts only if it prevents the service from responding to requests (not per-token latency spikes).

I'll add redlines to the doc and propose the hybrid ladder Ben suggested but tighten the cap to 40% for the monthly credit cap (we can negotiate upward if Synapse pushes). Also recommend we offer a one-time operational allowance (first month) if Synapse signs a 12-month Dedicated commitment to help them through cutover.

-Marissa
Director, Product Partnerships | Redwood Inference


From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Dave Allen <dave.allen@synapse.com>
Cc: Synapse Legal <legal@synapse.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 10 Aug 2026 16:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Draft SLA and questions on monthly remedy formula

Dave — thanks for the clear markup and questions. We've reviewed internally and can propose the following consolidated position to keep the procurement process moving: 

1) Availability: Redwood can commit to 99.95% monthly availability for Synapse if the contract is for a Dedicated capacity pool (reserved GPU capacity) in us-west-2. For standard hosted tiers, the availability commitment remains 99.9%.

2) Remedy ladder: we recommend a hybrid ladder with worked examples. Proposed bands:
  - >=99.95%: 0% credit
  - 99.90%–99.949%: 3% of monthly invoice
  - 99.00%–99.899%: 10% of monthly invoice
  - <99.00%: 25% of monthly invoice
  Monthly credit cap: 40% of monthly invoice. (We can revisit the cap if Synapse takes a 12-month Dedicated commitment; in that case we can offer a 50% cap for the first 90 days.)

3) Maintenance carve-out: scheduled maintenance (announced >=48 hours in advance) will be excluded from uptime calculations. We will limit scheduled maintenance to a maximum of 8 hours per month aggregate for production-impacting events and 4 hours max per event for minor upgrades. Emergency maintenance will be documented with a postmortem shared within 7 business days.

4) Regional failover: if Redwood initiates automated failover due to a regional outage, the time during failover will be counted as downtime; customer-initiated failover that results from Synapse changes (DNS, routing) will not count toward Redwood's downtime. Cross-region replication lag will only count if it causes an inability to serve requests.

5) Example calculation: if Synapse's monthly invoice is $100,000 and measured availability for August is 99.85% (downtime bucket 99.00–99.899%), the monthly credit would be 10% -> $10,000, subject to the 40% monthly cap.

Next steps: attached is a redlined SLA draft with the above edits (SLA_Synapse_v1_redlines.pdf) and a versioned doc (SLA_Synapse_v2.docx). Please review and let me know if you'd like a 30-minute call to walk through the bands and cap. If Synapse prefers the original pro-rata ladder, we can discuss trading on a committed volume/term to get closer to their values.

If legal prefers different phrasing for carve-outs or notices, loop them in and we'll iterate quickly.

Thanks — looking forward to finalizing.

Jordan Blake
Head of Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference

Attachments: SLA_Synapse_v1_redlines.pdf (pdf), SLA_Synapse_v2.docx (docx)

title: Seat smoothing, feature phasing, and mid-month tier swap policy
preview:
From: Amira Gomez <amira@lumenhealth.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 9 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Question on seat smoothing & feature phasing for pilot

Hi Chloe,

Thanks again for the demo last week. We're aligning internally on packaging for the pilot and had a couple of tactical questions that will affect procurement:

- Seat smoothing: can we pool seats across months and use 'carry-over' credits for under-utilized months, or is usage strictly bucketed per billing period?
- Mid-month tier swap/proration: if we up-tier mid-month for a new cohort, how do you prorate seat charges and feature add-ons?
- Feature phasing: can we stagger feature enablement (e.g., core inference first, then advanced reranking) and only pay for add-ons once enabled for a cohort?

I'm attaching the RFP excerpt. Can we get a short note with options and any standard credit math? If helpful, we can do a 30-min sync.

Thanks,

Amira Gomez
Sr. Procurement Manager
Lumen Health
Attachments: Lumen_RFP_Q3.pdf


From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
To: Amira Gomez <amira@lumenhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 9 Aug 2026 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Question on seat smoothing & feature phasing for pilot

Hi Amira —

Appreciate the RFP excerpt. Short answers up front, then specifics below:

- Seat smoothing: Yes — we offer a seat-smoothing option for pilots and agreed credits. It lets you pool committed seats across the billing window and apply negative variance as credits to the next period (up to 2 months carry-over by default).
- Mid-month tier swap/proration: We prorate based on time-weighted seats and any additive feature charges. Example math below.
- Feature phasing: Supported — features can be toggled per cohort via feature flags; billing starts when flags are enabled for that cohort.

Proposed options (high level):
1) Pilot Pool (recommended)
   - Commit to a monthly seat pool (e.g., 300 seats).
   - Unused seats converted to credits (up to 2 months carry-over).
   - Add-on features billed only when toggled on per cohort.
2) Flex Burst
   - Base commit smaller (e.g., 150 seats) + burst credits purchased or auto-burst billing at higher per-seat rate.
3) Mid-month Upgrade Guard (optional)
   - If you up-tier mid-month, we apply time-weighted proration and can issue a one-time credit equal to 15% of the incremental charge for that first cycle as a 'warm-up' concession for pilots.

Quick proration example:
- 30-day month, upgrade on day 16 from Standard (150 seats) to Pro (300 seats).
- Time-weighted charge = (15 days * 150 seats + 15 days * 300 seats) / 30 days.
- Feature add-ons prorated on days active.

I've attached a one-pager that summarizes seat smoothing and the math. If this aligns, Ben/ Priyom — can you review recommended credit percentages (we typically offer 10-20% for pilots) and confirm pricing for DL-2026-0789?

Best,
Chloe

---
Chloe Martin
Account Executive
Redwood Inference
Attachments: seat_smoothing_onepager.pdf


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Amira Gomez <amira@lumenhealth.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 10 Aug 2026 09:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Question on seat smoothing & feature phasing for pilot

Hi Amira,

Thanks Chloe — quick pricing scenarios for DL-2026-0789 (all USD):

Scenario A — Pilot Pool
 - Commit: 300 seats/month
 - Unit rate: $0.015/seat
 - Effective monthly spend: $4,500
 - Carry-over credits: up to 2 months
 - Pilot concession: 12% off first 3 months (applied on net spend)

Scenario B — Flex Burst
 - Commit: 150 seats/month
 - Unit rate: $0.017/seat
 - Burst rate: $0.025/seat when auto-burst
 - Pilot concession: 8% on base commit

Mid-month swap treatment: we do time-weighted proration by seat-days; if Lumen prefers a simpler approach we can apply a midpoint billing adjustment (charge full day at new tier) but the math above is standard. Feature flags: enabled per cohort; billing begins on enablement date.

If you want the 15% 'warm-up' credit that Chloe mentioned, we'd need a 12-month commit to justify that concession — otherwise 10% for a 6-month pilot is within standard flexibility.

I'm free 8/11 10–11am PT if you want to lock in a sync. I'll include a draft term sheet after the call and a DocuSign when we align.

Ben
Account Executive
Redwood Inference


From: Amira Gomez <amira@lumenhealth.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 10 Aug 2026 15:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Question on seat smoothing & feature phasing for pilot

Thanks — that all helps.

We can do a 6-month pilot with an option to extend to 12 months if metrics are met. For procurement to move forward we need:
- Clarified proration example in writing (we prefer time-weighted seat-days, so please include the formula in the term sheet).
- Confirmation that feature flags can be gated per cohort and that billing is cohort-specific.
- Confirmation on the maximum carry-over months (2 months ok).

8/11 10am PT works for us. I'll send a calendar invite.

Best,
Amira
Sr. Procurement Manager
Lumen Health

Subject: Next steps & procurement framing — Redwood for Novus HealthTech

Hi Alyssa —

Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap from our call and proposed next steps so everyone is aligned. I wanted to make sure we captured the pain, success criteria, and the procurement path before we loop in legal/procurement formally.

Pain points you shared
- Clinical teams are spending ~8–12 hours/week manually triaging patient notes for coding exceptions; accuracy drops with noisy EHR text.
- Current homegrown model has high variance and unpredictable latency at scale.
- Tight data residency / audit requirements for PHI processing.

Current stack
- EHR: Epic; pre-processing: internal Python pipeline; model: in-house fine-tuned transformer (CPU inference behind a queue).
- On-prem data retention and SIEM integration required for audit logs.

Success criteria (what you told us)
1) Reduce triage time by >=40% across pilot workload.
2) Median inference latency <150ms for single-record calls when under pilot load.
3) Clear audit logs and SOC2-like evidence for the pilot scope.

Procurement & decision process (as discussed)
- Primary decision owner: Alyssa Turner (Clinical Ops).
- Procurement lead: Evan Liu (Procurement) — expects an initial commercial review + standard PO / SOW path.
- Legal: Novus legal will need a standard data processing addendum; timeline target: internal approvals in 3–4 weeks if pilot terms are standard.

Initial commercial guardrails (we proposed)
- Pilot term: 8 weeks, capped to 100k tokens/month (estimate) with a fixed pilot fee.
- If pilot succeeds, propose a phased Dedicated reservation + per-token overage.
- We can offer a limited pilot discount and an SLA uplift for Dedicated in scope of production rollout.

Attachments I’m sharing
- SOW-draft-novus-pilot-v1.pdf (pilot scope + acceptance criteria)
- pricing-estimate-novus.csv (pilot fee & post-pilot illustrative pricing)
- security-quickpack.pdf (SOC2 summary + audit artifacts we can provide)

Proposed next steps
1) I’ll share the SOW and pricing (attached) — please review by Thu 8/12.
2) Can you confirm Evan (procurement) as the person who will handle the PO/SOW?
3) If that’s correct, we’ll loop in our deals team and legal to prepare a pilot SOW + DPA template for Novus review.
4) Optional: a short tech deep-dive with Laura and your infra/security lead to confirm logging and network constraints (20–30m).

If helpful I can also send a short one-pager that maps success criteria to measurable telemetry (latency, per-record accuracy, audit logs).

Best,
Ben

Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference

title: Annual security engagement: evidence transfer, staging access & proposed windows
preview:
From: Samuel Ortiz <sam.ortiz@acmeretail.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-04T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Annual pen test / SOW renewal — initial details

Ben —

We need to get the annual security engagement on the calendar. We have a contractual renewal window coming up and procurement asked me to collect a couple of technical questions before they will sign a new SOW:

- What evidence will OliveSec need from us (logs, config dumps, test accounts)?
- Will tests require access to our staging CIDR or just individual test hosts?
- Any constraints around data handling for PII discovered during testing?

Olivia at OliveSec is the vendor lead (cc'd on this thread). If possible, we want the technical checklist and a proposed attack window so procurement can align the PO and insurance review. Proposed windows we can support right now are: Sept 20–24 or Nov 8–12.

Thanks,
Sam
Sr. Product Manager — Acme Retail

---
Attachment: previous_SOW_2025.pdf (reference)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Grant <olivia@olivesec.com>, Samuel Ortiz <sam.ortiz@acmeretail.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-04T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Annual pen test / SOW renewal — initial details

Samuel, Olivia —

Thanks for starting this. Quick answers and requests so procurement can move:

1) We need a precise evidence requirements list (log retention windows, sample syslog export, auth flow diagrams). Procurement and InfoSec will not sign without this.
2) Staging access: we prefer scoped host/IP access to a dedicated staging CIDR. Any plan that requires production credentials or broad network access will need an elevated approval.
3) Data handling: Redwood handles PII in accordance with our data classification policy. Please highlight any vendor-specific retention/replication steps — procurement requires an insurance cert and SOC2 Type II report.

Olivia — can you send a draft SOW + an evidence checklist (spreadsheet preferred)? If you have a sample DocuSign envelope link for the SOW, include that too.

Proposed next steps: vendor sends SOW + checklist; we do an internal review (Tech + Procurement + Legal); then agree a final window. For planning, tentatively favor the Sept 20–24 window but we'll confirm after reviewing the SOW.

Ben
Director, Infrastructure Security
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: internal_prechecklist_template.xlsx

From: Olivia Grant <olivia@olivesec.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Samuel Ortiz <sam.ortiz@acmeretail.com>
Cc: procurement@olivesec.com
Date: 2026-08-05T08:22:00-07:00
Subject: OliveSec SOW draft + evidence checklist

Hi Ben / Sam —

Thanks — attaching our draft SOW and an evidence requirements sheet. Highlights below:

- Scope: authenticated web app testing (OAuth + SSO flows), internal API surface (service-to-service), mobile backend (if enabled), and optional 2-day red team add-on.
- Evidence we request: 30 days of auth logs (CSV export), deployment manifest (k8s YAMLs sanitized), sample app user accounts for testing, network ACLs for staging CIDR.
- Data handling: OliveSec will not persist any PII beyond the engagement; any findings with PII are redacted before storage. We also carry a cyber-insurance policy and SOC2 Type II; cert attached.
- Proposed windows: Sept 20–24 preferred; alternate Nov 8–12.
- DocuSign: https://docusign.com/Acme/Envelope/12345 (SOW v1)

Notes on pre-test exercise: we recommend a 2-week pre-test runbook rehearsal with your on-call engineer to validate access and sampling.

We attached: OliveSec_SOW_v1.pdf, evidence_requirements_sheet.xlsx, OliveSec_insurance_cert.pdf

Happy to hop on a quick 30-min sync if that helps.

—Olivia Grant
OliveSec — Senior Engagement Manager
olivia@olivesec.com

---
Attachment: OliveSec_SOW_v1.pdf
Attachment: evidence_requirements_sheet.xlsx
Attachment: OliveSec_insurance_cert.pdf

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 6 Aug 2026 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Fwd: BlueCore MSA - liability cap / IP indemnity redlines

Ava / Karthik — thanks for the context. Read their redline. Summary + recommended compromise below.

Summary of their ask:
- Carve-out IP indemnity and any damages (direct/indirect) arising from IP claims from the liability cap.
- No dollar limit on IP indemnity; they want Redwood to remain fully responsible for IP infringement.

Risks for Redwood:
- Unlimited exposure on high statutory damages (rare) or large settlements.
- If we accept a full unlimited carve-out, it sets a precedent for other enterprise deals.

Recommended approach (two-part):
1) Preserve a carve-out for IP indemnity BUT limit remedy to: (a) amounts actually paid in a final judgment or settlement; and (b) Redwood's obligation to replace/modify the infringing component or procure a license; and (c) Redwood will not be liable for speculative or punitive damages tied to IP claims.

2) Cap the monetary exposure for IP indemnity at the greater of: (i) the liability cap in the Order Form (12 months' fees), or (ii) an amount equal to 3x the annual fees paid under the Order Form. This creates an excess layer but not unlimited liability.

3) Add a requirement that BlueCore must (a) provide prompt written notice of claim, (b) provide relevant documentation, and (c) allow Redwood to control defense and settlement (subject to reasonable consent rights for BlueCore re: admissions).

4) If BlueCore insists on no cap, propose an alternative: escrowed insurance/bonding or a capped indemnity with explicit carve-out for injunctive relief (i.e., equitable remedies allowed without monetary carve-out).

Suggested contract language (short form):
"Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, Redwood's aggregate liability for claims arising out of or relating to IP infringement shall be limited to the greater of (A) the Liability Cap set forth in the Order Form or (B) three times the Fees paid in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim; provided, however, that this limitation shall not apply to amounts Redwood is obligated to pay pursuant to a final judgment or settlement in an IP infringement claim, and Redwood's obligation shall be limited to amounts actually paid and any reasonable costs to obtain a license or replace the infringing component."

Plain English rationale to give the customer: we accept a carve-out for IP but need a sensible, bounded exposure.

Next step: Ava — can you confirm whether BlueCore is likely to accept a multiplier approach (3x) or whether they are bound by procurement policy that forbids any cap exclusion? If the latter, we'll need to escalate.

I can draft a redline to send them if you want.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Senior Counsel, Contracts | Redwood Inference

title: Private VPC: proposed control-plane/data-plane lane separation and egress termination
preview:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@plumecorp.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: PlumeCorp Infra <infra@plumecorp.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Initial ask: private VPC egress + control/data separation

Ava — thanks for taking this. We’re starting our internal review for PlumeCorp’s private VPC deployment and need to lock the control-plane/data-plane separation and a hardened egress termination plan before we schedule the preflight. High level asks: 

- Confirm whether Redwood requires a dedicated control-plane endpoint inside the customer VPC or if a controlled public egress with mTLS and IP allowlists is acceptable.
- We need reference schematics that show NAT/egress termination points, HA expectations, and preferred observability hooks (span tags, metrics).
- Any special requirements for KV-cache prefixing or cross-tenant routing that might require on-prem proxying.

Can you send a short proposal and a schematic we can bring to our infra review on Friday? We’re under a compliance window (internal audit) so we’ll need evidence of traffic zoning and egress filtering.

Thanks,
Rafael Mendes
Head of Platform — PlumeCorp

(Attachment: compliance-extract.csv)


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@plumecorp.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Aug 2026 13:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: private VPC egress + control/data separation

Rafael — thanks for the clear ask. Attached is a short proposal and a schematic (controlplane-egress-proposal.pdf and vpc-schematic-v2.png) that outline two supported patterns:

1) Dedicated control-plane endpoint (recommended for high-compliance customers)
   - Control-plane: customer-deployed control-plane connector in a management subnet that terminates mTLS inbound from Redwood control nodes. No customer data plane traffic flows through this path.
   - Data-plane: separate tenant pod(s) in private subnets with NAT egress through a customer-managed egress appliance or cloud NAT.

2) Shared public-control with hardened egress (supported for lower-compliance customers)
   - Control-plane: public endpoint with strict mTLS + client cert pinning + IP allowlist. Requires documented allowlist windows for failover.
   - Data-plane: same as above but with additional per-tenant egress filtering enforced by Redwood sidecar and prefix-aware KV caching.

Key notes and differences in the attached PDF:
- Egress termination options (customer-managed vs Redwood-managed) and audit evidence we can provide for each.
- Observability hooks: span sampling at token ingress, per-route token cost labels, egress flow metrics and sample traces; we can drop an MRTG-style baseline for you.
- Canary approach: we propose a two-week canary using the canary-checklist.xlsx attached; it validates KVCACHE correctness and egress rule fidelity before full cutover.

Next steps I suggest:
- Quick 30-min design readout with your infra team (we can do Wed or Thu).
- If you want dedicated control-plane, we’ll provide an ops-runbook and a signed MOU for the connector deployment.

I’ll wait for a preferred time — looping in Karthik and Jonas for the detailed networking bits.

—
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com | +1 415 555 0123
(Attachments: controlplane-egress-proposal.pdf, vpc-schematic-v2.png, canary-checklist.xlsx)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@plumecorp.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 5 Aug 2026 10:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: private VPC egress + control/data separation

Rafael / team — quick follow-ups from a networking perspective after reviewing Ava’s proposal:

- If you choose dedicated control-plane (pattern 1), we strongly recommend a three-node connector cluster in your management subnet with an autoscaling policy disabled during initial rollouts (manual scale-up during emergency). This reduces transient certificate churn and makes auditing simpler.
- Egress fingerprinting: please ensure your NAT appliance preserves source port mapping or provides 1:1 SNAT for labeled control traffic; our observability relies on stable 5-tuple hashing for traces.
- MTU and fragmentation: we hit issues when customers had 1500 MTU plus overlay encapsulation. Recommend 1400 MTU for data-plane pods if you intend to use IP-in-IP or GRE for tenant isolation.
- Firewall rules: allow Redwood control IPs + Cloudflare (if using our public endpoint) on TCP 443 for mTLS. We’ll share a JSON snippet of the recommended security group/ACLs — attaching for your infra team.

I’ll join the readout. Jonas — can you add a short diagram showing where KV-cache prefixes are stored/how they're invalidated in the dedicated connector pattern?

-Karthik

(Attachment: redwood-acl-snippet.json)

From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@plumecorp.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 6 Aug 2026 16:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: private VPC egress + control/data separation

Thanks — Rafael, we did a quick internal sketch and a short list of action items for your Friday review:

Action items (Redwood + PlumeCorp):
- PlumeCorp: provision management subnet with three control connector nodes behind the supplied ACLs (due: 2026-08-10).
- PlumeCorp: confirm egress appliance model and whether it supports 1:1 SNAT (due: 2026-08-09).
- Redwood: provide final MOU and ops-runbook for connector install (due: 2026-08-07).
- Joint: run the canary checklist (two-week window) once connector is installed; target start 2026-08-15.

Design clarifications:
- KV-cache: in the dedicated connector pattern the KV-store mapping is local to the connector; invalidation occurs via a signed lifecycle event from Redwood control when a model version rollout happens. We added a short appendix in the PDF.
- Egress auditing: we’ll supply flow-sampled traces and a compressed daily evidence bundle (TLS SNI mapping and egress IP table) for audit.

If that looks good I’ll draft the runbook headings for the MOU. Rafael — any immediate blocker on your side?

Cheers,
Jonas Weber
Principal Network Architect, Redwood Inference

Rafael / team — quick follow-ups from a networking perspective after reviewing Ava’s proposal:

- If you choose dedicated control-plane (pattern 1), we strongly recommend a three-node connector cluster in your management subnet with an autoscaling policy disabled during initial rollouts (manual scale-up during emergency). This reduces transient certificate churn and makes auditing simpler.
- Egress fingerprinting: please ensure your NAT appliance preserves source port mapping or provides 1:1 SNAT for labeled control traffic; our observability relies on stable 5-tuple hashing for traces.
- MTU and fragmentation: we hit issues when customers had 1500 MTU plus overlay encapsulation. Recommend 1400 MTU for data-plane pods if you intend to use IP-in-IP or GRE for tenant isolation.
- Firewall rules: allow Redwood control IPs + Cloudflare (if using our public endpoint) on TCP 443 for mTLS. We’ll share a JSON snippet of the recommended security group/ACLs — attaching for your infra team.

I’ll join the readout. Jonas — can you add a short diagram showing where KV-cache prefixes are stored/how they're invalidated in the dedicated connector pattern?

-Karthik

(Attachment: redwood-acl-snippet.json)

Subject: Re: Initial ask: private VPC egress + control/data separation

Action items (Redwood + PlumeCorp):
- PlumeCorp: provision management subnet with three control connector nodes behind the supplied ACLs (due: 2026-08-10).
- PlumeCorp: confirm egress appliance model and whether it supports 1:1 SNAT (due: 2026-08-09).
- Redwood: provide final MOU and ops-runbook for connector install (due: 2026-08-07).
- Joint: run the canary checklist (two-week window) once connector is installed; target start 2026-08-15.

Design clarifications:
- KV-cache: in the dedicated connector pattern the KV-store mapping is local to the connector; invalidation occurs via a signed lifecycle event from Redwood control when a model version rollout happens. We added a short appendix in the PDF.
- Egress auditing: we’ll supply flow-sampled traces and a compressed daily evidence bundle (TLS SNI mapping and egress IP table) for audit.

Subject: SummitAI pilot scope confirmation & SDK asks

Hi Marcus / Priya —

Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap from our discovery so we’re all aligned and to make the next steps explicit. I’ve attached a short pilot outline and our architecture diagram.

Pain points you called out:
- Production latency spikes when users run long-context retrieval flows (avg 800–1,200ms currently for single-turn responses).
- Cost unpredictability at scale for embedding + rerank pipelines.
- Need for structured JSON outputs downstream for ticket triage (current heuristic parsing fails ~18% of cases).
- Desire to trial function-calling style integrations with streaming responses to improve perceived responsiveness.

Goals for the pilot (6-week pilot):
1) Validate latency and cost improvements using Redwood batching + KV prefix caching on 1M monthly requests equivalent.
2) Evaluate embeddings + reranking quality lift on your relevance benchmark (we’ll run our eval harness against your test set).
3) Deliver a streaming/function-calling proof-of-concept that returns structured JSON for ticket fields (priority, category, assignee).

Proposed scope / deliverables:
- Sandbox access to Redwood Hosted endpoint + a small dedicated GPU instance for baseline runs.
- SDK integration work: function-calling examples (node/python), streaming client sample, and a structured-output prompt template.
- Weekly syncs (Wednesdays) and a mid-pilot checkpoint to decide on SLOs.
- SOW draft (attached) that covers implementation support and a small usage commitment if we move to Dedicated.

Immediate asks from your side:
- Please confirm the two contacts who will be tech POCs and provide a sanitized sample of 1,000 anonymized ticket texts (CSV) for embeddings evaluation.
- Confirm whether SOC 2 evidence will be required for sandbox access.
- If you want, we can start the SOW and NDA in parallel — Ben can own SOW drafting.

Attachments: SummitAI-pilot-outline.pdf, redwood-arch-diagram.png, SOW-draft.docx

Next steps (I’ll take these unless you prefer otherwise):
- Ben will draft a lean SOW and return it by EOD Friday.
- Karthik will schedule a 60-minute tech deep-dive next week (we recommended Tue/Wed morning PT).
- Marcus/Priya to share POC contacts + sample dataset.

Thanks — looking forward to moving this to a pilot.

Chloe Martin
Sr. Account Executive | Redwood Inference
