title: Tenant egress & IP allowlist — handoff and preflight
preview:
From: Amelia Torres <amelia.torres@brightline.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 02 Aug 2026 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Tenant egress & IP allowlist — handoff and preflight

Hi Aditya / Marissa,

We're preparing network-side changes for our private deployment and need to hand off the egress/IP allowlist items to our infra team. Before we open a ticket with our network ops, quick clarifying questions:

- What exact IPs or CIDR blocks will Redwood require for outbound traffic from our tenant?
- Do you require a fixed source NAT (SNAT) from our side, or do you accept a NATed pool from our gateway?
- Any specific port ranges or protocols besides HTTPS (443) that need to be opened (e.g., health checks, metrics, agent checks)?

I've attached our current allowlist export template which we can populate once we confirm. Goal is to complete network changes this week so we can start smoke tests next Monday.

Thanks —
Amelia
Network Lead | Brightline Health
amelia.torres@brightline.com
Attachment: egress_ip_allowlist.csv (CSV)



From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Amelia Torres <amelia.torres@brightline.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 02 Aug 2026 13:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Tenant egress & IP allowlist — handoff and preflight

Hi Amelia — thanks for the clear list. High level answers up front, then an attached preflight checklist we use for every private deployment.

- Redwood outbound endpoints: we use a small, stable set of IPs for control-plane and telemetry. For Brightline we will provide the exact /32 list once you confirm the environment name; placeholder below.
- SNAT: we prefer a stable source IP per tenant (SNAT) so our servers can maintain simple allowlist rules. If your gateway provides a Pooled NAT, we can still work with it but will need the exact ephemeral pool and a short-lived testing cadence.
- Ports/protocols: primary requirement is HTTPS (443). Additionally:
  - TCP 443 outgoing to our control endpoints (required)
  - TCP 443 incoming is not required unless you run a webhook; for agent callbacks we use outbound calls from Redwood.
  - ICMP for simple ping health checks is optional; we rely on HTTPS endpoints for readiness.

Please see the attached "env-validation-checklist.pdf" — it includes the fields we need filled (vpc id, nat gateway allocation, subnet CIDRs, peering info, DNS entries, and ACL rules). I pasted the quick allowlist example below.

Sample egress list (placeholder):
- 52.23.101.19/32  (control.redwood-inference.com)
- 34.89.210.45/32  (metrics.redwood-inference.com)
- 18.197.64.12/32  (artifact-downloads.redwood-inference.com)

Next steps:
1) If you can attach the completed CSV with your VPC NAT IP(s), we'll validate and return the final /32 list.
2) Ben (cc) will outline test steps for connectivity and a simple curl-based smoke test.

Thanks —
Aditya Rao
Manager, Solutions Engineering | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com
Attachment: env-validation-checklist.pdf (PDF)



From: Amelia Torres <amelia.torres@brightline.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 03 Aug 2026 08:14:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Tenant egress & IP allowlist — handoff and preflight

Aditya — attaching our filled CSV (egress_ip_allowlist.csv). We have two NAT IPs from our primary region and one secondary for failover. We can provide a static SNAT if that is preferred; ops prefers configuring a single elastic IP per NAT gateway.

Questions from our ops lead:
- Do you validate TLS client certs or IP-based allowlisting is sufficient?
- Will Redwood initiate connections to any ports other than 443 for the agent/heartbeat?

Proposed go-live window: Thu Aug 6th (after your validation). Let me know if that works.

Cheers,
Amelia
Attachment: egress_ip_allowlist.csv (CSV)



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Amelia Torres <amelia.torres@brightline.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 03 Aug 2026 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Tenant egress & IP allowlist — handoff and preflight

Hi Amelia — Ben on infra here. Quick technical clarifications and recommended rules you can hand to your firewall team: 

1) TLS/client auth: for this deployment we do not require mTLS at the network layer. IP allowlisting + TLS server validation is sufficient. Application-layer tokens are used for per-tenant auth. If you prefer mTLS we can support it but that requires cert distribution and is out of scope for this initial cutover.

2) Ports: only outbound TCP 443 is needed. For the smoke tests we also reference 443 for health endpoints. No inbound ports required from Redwood IPs.

3) Suggested firewall ACL entries (egress allowed):
  - ALLOW tcp from <tenant-subnet> to 52.23.101.19/32 port 443  (control API)
  - ALLOW tcp from <tenant-subnet> to 34.89.210.45/32 port 443  (metrics/telemetry)
  - ALLOW tcp from <tenant-subnet> to 18.197.64.12/32 port 443  (artifacts)

4) NAT guidance: single elastic IP per NAT gateway is ideal. If you have a HA pair with active/standby, please provide both IPs and mark the active one – we will accept both but prefer a stable primary.

5) Smoke test steps (run from a host in the tenant subnet):
  - curl -v https://control.redwood-inference.com/healthz (should return 200 and JSON with "status":"ok")
  - curl -v https://metrics.redwood-inference.com/ping (200 expected)
  - traceroute -T -p 443 control.redwood-inference.com (confirm routing path)

If you'd like, we can run the smoke tests with you on a quick call. Otherwise, once you confirm ACLs and we've validated your CSV, we'll issue the final /32 list and sign off.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Infra Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com



From: Amelia Torres <amelia.torres@brightline.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 03 Aug 2026 09:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Tenant egress & IP allowlist — handoff and preflight

Ben / Aditya — this is great, thank you. Ops confirms they can set a single elastic IP per NAT gateway; I've updated the CSV to mark the active IP. We'll schedule the change window for Thu Aug 6 at 02:00 - 04:00 PDT. Can we do a 30-minute test/validation call at 03:00 PDT? If you confirm, I'll create the change request and share the ticket (HS-987654) and invite the teams.

Also confirming: no inbound rules required and IP allowlist + TLS server auth is acceptable for initial rollout. We'll revisit mTLS in Q4 if needed.

Thanks —
Amelia
Network Lead | Brightline Health
amelia.torres@brightline.com

title: Dedicated capacity sizing: SKU recommendations & tradeoffs for Acme Health deal
preview:
From: Samir Khan <samir@acmehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Health Infra <infra@acmehealth.com>
Date: Sun, 2 Aug 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Dedicated capacity: expected traffic profile & SKU guidance request

Hi Ben / Marissa,

We're getting close to contract signature for the telemetry ingest + summarization feature. Before we finalize committed capacity we want a short read on the right GPU mix for our Dedicated pool. High level constraints:
- Peak concurrent requests: ~480 requests/minute for the web tier (p50 latency target 250ms)
- Typical prompt lengths: 512 tokens, with ~5% of requests reaching 4k tokens (long-run summaries)
- Expected steady QPS: 4 qps average, bursting to ~8 qps during events
- Budget: want to keep committed monthly spend predictable; prefer a mix that avoids large over-provisioning but has clear upgrade path

Specific ask: given Redwood SKUs (H100, A100, L4) and the workload above, what mix would you recommend for month-1 committed capacity vs a 3-month runway? Memory/throughput tradeoffs and any quantization/latency knobs we should plan for would be helpful.

Thanks,
Samir
Samir Khan
VP, Platform Engineering
Acme Health



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Samir Khan <samir@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 2 Aug 2026 15:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity: expected traffic profile & SKU guidance request

Samir — thanks for the clear profile. Attached is a quick sizing spreadsheet (acme-dedicated-sizing-v1.xlsx) with three starter configurations and estimated throughput/costs. High-level guidance below.

Short summary (starter commit recommendations):
- Conservative baseline (cost-sensitive): 6x L4 nodes. Good for short-context (<= 1k tokens), best unit economics for high fan-out web traffic. Lower memory but very cost-efficient when most requests are short.
- Balanced (recommended for this deal): 3x A100 + 2x L4. A100s handle mixed-length requests and longer KV caches; L4s absorb the short tail and keep unit cost down.
- Performance / large-context heavy: 4x H100. If the 4k token tail grows or you run many simultaneous long summarizations, H100s provide headroom for larger batch sizes and bigger context windows.

Tradeoffs to note:
- Memory: H100 > A100 > L4. If you expect many cached contexts or models with large activations, prefer H100 or A100 (with 80GB variants where available).
- Latency: For small requests L4 often has lower p50 cost per token but higher p99 variability under bursty loads — we rely on autoscaling/burst policies to smooth that.
- Cost-per-token: L4 lowest, A100 mid, H100 highest but best for high-throughput long-context work.

Operational suggestions:
- Start with the Balanced mix and enable prefix/KV caching on A100s for the summarization flows.
- Configure burst capacity to allow autoscaling into host-pools (A100/H100) for durable p99 latency during events.
- Quantization: for text-gen models, int8/4 quantization is viable on L4 and A100 for most customers; H100 gives more headroom for FP16 paths with higher fidelity on long outputs.

I'll loop in Dev for kernel/throughput knobs and cost estimates per 1M tokens. Can you confirm: target SLA (p99) and whether you need a hard guarantee on cold-starts?

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

Attachment: acme-dedicated-sizing-v1.xlsx (sizing workbook, tabs: workloads, sku-comparison, monthly-commit-scenarios)


From: Dev Patel <dev@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Samir Khan <samir@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 2 Aug 2026 16:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity: expected traffic profile & SKU guidance request

Adding a few technical details from runtime side — quick notes rather than exhaustive benching.

- Sequence length distribution matters more than average. If 95% are ~512 and 5% are 4k, plan for capacity that can splice long requests onto H100/A100 while L4s cover the short ones.
- KV cache & batching: Enable per-model prefix caching and 8-16 request batching on A100/H100. Continuous batching on L4 is less effective for long contexts.
- Memory configs: A100 80GB variants are significantly more flexible for pinned model shards and larger MVKV caches. H100 scales better for multi-tenant high batch sizes and for larger models with big kv state.
- Quantization: We recommend trying int8 first on L4 for cost savings. For the summarization model we saw <2% output drift on int8 relatively consistently; if you need bit-exact reproduction use H100 FP16 paths.
- Kernel/compilation: we can pre-warm kernels for your production model variants during the onboarding window; this reduces p99 by ~30% for long outputs.

Estimated ops numbers (very rough):
- Single H100 (80GB) can sustain ~1800 tokens/sec for long-context summarization workload (4k) with batching tuned.
- Single A100 (40/80GB) ~900-1200 tokens/sec depending on batch shape and quantization.
- Single L4 ~250-500 tokens/sec optimized for short sequences.

I'll attach a short one-pager we can share with procurement that formalizes these numbers.

- Dev
Dev Patel
Inference Infrastructure, Redwood Inference

Attachment: rdw_sku-comparison-onepager.pdf

3) Timeline: if we lock month-1 commit today, how long until Dedicated pool is provisioned and we can start onboarding models?

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>

Samir — quick answers inline and next steps.

1) Ballpark committed cost (Balanced: 3x A100 + 2x L4) — monthly committed estimate: USD 112,000 / month (this is a starting commit; see spreadsheet for per-hour burn and optional burst pricing). We'll include a 10% onboarding credit for the first month in the SOW.

2) SLA (p99 <= 350ms for <=512 tokens) — achievable if we:
   - Pin the short-request route to L4 frontends with an autoscaled backing pool on A100s for heavier tails.
   - Enable kernel pre-warm and prefix caching.

   - Provisioning window: 7-10 business days for Dedicated pool in our us-west region (we'll prioritize on exec sign-off).
   - Model onboarding & perf tuning: 5 business days after capacity is live for first model (includes kernel pre-warm and quantization trial).

title: Seed program credits — eligibility details and SOW request
preview:
From: Alyssa Chen <alyssa@nimbusai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rohan Gupta <rohan@acceleratorhub.org>
Date: Tue, 27 Jul 2026 17:08:00 -0700
Subject: Seed program credits for NimbusAI?

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — the latency numbers looked great for our retrieval+generation flow. We're an AI startup (building conversational agents for telescope data) and we've been accepted into the Horizon Seed Accelerator. As part of the program we get partner credits, and Rohan suggested we talk to Redwood about a supplemental seed grant to help stretch our pilot into Q1.

Quick ask: could Redwood provide a credits allocation (or trial extension) in the $8k–$12k range to cover our pre-production runs for 3 months? We have an MVP demo ready and a simple questionnaire attached that outlines expected token volume and security needs.

I've attached the questionnaire (nimbus_questionnaire.xlsx) and a text file with the demo link (nimbus_mvp_demo_link.txt). Happy to hop on a call with your team this week.

Best,
Alyssa Chen
Co-founder & CTO, NimbusAI Labs

--
Attachment: nimbus_questionnaire.xlsx (xlsx, 38KB)
Attachment: nimbus_mvp_demo_link.txt (txt, 1KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alyssa Chen <alyssa@nimbusai.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 28 Jul 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Seed program credits for NimbusAI?

Hi Alyssa — thanks for sending this over and congrats on Horizon. Quick read-through of the questionnaire looks promising. A couple quick qualification questions so we can route this to the credit committee: 

- Is Nimbus currently in a paid pilot with any other inference provider? (yes/no)
- Expected monthly token volume for August–October (ranges are fine).
- Do you require a Private deployment or is hosted API sufficient?

If hosted is fine and volumes are within a 5M–25M token window, we can usually approve a promotional credit grant that surfaces as account credit with a 3-month expiry. I'll loop in Marissa to align on program banding and Evelyn for any exceptions to the standard terms.

If you want, drop times for a 30-min sync — happy to do 10:30–11:30am PT tomorrow.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Senior Growth Manager | Redwood Inference

--

Ben / Marissa — initial triage notes below. Samantha, leaving you on for visibility.

What I see:
- Change window: 07/29 08:58 PDT — catalog pipeline swapped GrayFox's default embeddings model from gf-embed-v2-q8 to gf-embed-v3-q4_legacy on hosted.
- Immediately after the swap we see a change in tokenization and a higher average sequence-length for our internal batching path which is causing increased batch times.
- KV cache hit-rate dropped for warm requests which pushed more work into the slow path.
- There is also a mismatch in the runtime kernel selection for q4 runtime on the nodes serving GrayFox (we're seeing a fallback to a conservative kernel for some sequence lengths).

Evidence: trace_spans_0729.pb attached; sample latency histograms linked to the dashboard.

Suggested immediate mitigations:
1) Revert GrayFox traffic to gf-embed-v2-q8 (rollback plan attached). This is lowest-risk and should restore previous p95/p99.
2) If rollback is not acceptable, we can temporarily pin GrayFox to dedicated pool with tuned kernel flags (needs infra coordination and capacity).
3) Post-mortem: fix catalog routing logic to avoid swapping quantization profile without staged canary for existing heavy tenants.

I'll start a rollback in the next 20 minutes unless I get a different directive.

-rishi

Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 14:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo recap + next steps (pilot scope & timeline)

Hi Sanjay —

Thanks again for the call today. Quick recap from my side and a couple of asks so we can lock timing before quarter close: 

- What we covered: 2-week pilot (secure workspace + up to 50k tokens/day), Redwood runtime with prefix caching, and integration options for your ingestion pipeline.
- Your stated goal: prove 3x latency improvement on key generation paths and validate cost profile for scale.
- Open items for Frontline to confirm: budget owner, procurement timeline, and whether SOC2 evidence will be required before pilot kick-off.

Attached: proposal-draft.pdf (scope + SOW draft), mvp-costs.xlsx (cost estimates for options A/B/C), and a screenshot of the trial config we discussed (redwood-trial-config.png).

Ask: can you confirm (a) who signs the pilot SOW, and (b) whether the steering committee meets before July 31 so we can make an EoQ slot? If that timeline doesn't work we can propose a scaled scope that fits an expedited approval.

If helpful I can book a 20-min sync with your procurement and technical lead — here’s my calendar: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=abc123

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.ai | (415) 555-0142

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: partnerships@edgecraft.io
Cc: marissa.cole@redwood.com
Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Partnership: integration onboarding & revenue routing

Hi Aisha / Edgecraft team —

Great meeting last week. As discussed, we’re excited about a joint go-to-market where Edgecraft bundles a Redwood connector inside your managed offering. To make the commercial side simple for your sales team, can we align on two quick things so we can scope the technical onboarding work:  

1) Revenue routing model: do you prefer referral (one-time credit) or reseller/rev-share (ongoing split)? We typically support both; rev-share examples below.
2) Lead registration: what intake fields can we require to qualify for partner credit (e.g., account name, HubSpot ID, first contact date)?

I’ve attached a short draft rev-share schedule for discussion (edgecraft-revshare-proposal-v1.pdf) and a technical checklist so our integration and your engineering team can scope a joint demo/POC (partner-technical-checklist.xlsx).

Next step: if you’re good to explore rev-share, can we schedule a 30-minute sync with your alliances lead + our Partner Ops (Marissa) + Solutions (Jonas) this week?

Thanks — I’ll loop in Marissa now.

Best,
Ben Carter
Sr. Partnerships Manager
Redwood Inference
Attachment: edgecraft-revshare-proposal-v1.pdf (1 page)
Attachment: partner-technical-checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet)

- Minimum contract term for a rev-share? Our procurement asks about 12 months minimum for sustained payouts.

Aisha — thanks. Quick answers and suggested defaults from our side so the call can be efficient:

Ben

Subject: RE: Partnership: integration onboarding & revenue routing

Team —

Thanks for the fast turnaround. Attached is a one-pager mutual action plan (MMP) and a rev-share payout CSV that maps to three buckets: initial sale (year 1), renewal (year 2+), and professional services (integration fees). High-level recommendations:  

- Rev-share split (suggested / starting point): 20% for referral credit (one-time) OR 12% ongoing on-recurring revenue for reseller model; escalator to 15% after $500k ARR through Edgecraft in a rolling 12 months.
- Lead registration: HubSpot deal ID + first contact email + signed SOW; registration locks for 180 days unless the account converts earlier.
- Minimum payout term: we can honor a 12-month payout schedule for booked deals; Redwood will remit monthly with a 30-day payment term after invoice.
- Payouts: Redwood will provide monthly commission reports and tokenized invoice statements; payments via ACH or wire.

Action items for the call:
- Marley (Edgecraft) to confirm whether they need a reseller agreement vs referral.
- Jonas to cover technical onboarding SOW and estimated dev days (see checklist).
- Priya to review any regulatory clauses (data residency, logging).

Attachments: mmp_revshare_table.csv, edgecraft-revshare-proposal-v1.pdf, partner-technical-checklist.xlsx

I’ll also drop the MMP to a shared Drive (link below) and a DocuSign skeleton for signature capture after we agree commercial terms.

Shared Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcDk-edgecraft-partner
DocuSign (skeleton): https://app.docusign.com/Signing/XYZ-EDGE-2026

Marissa Cole

Thanks — excited to get started.

title: Consolidated QA kit onboarding plan + dataset retention windows
preview:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Maya Gupta <maya@chronicle.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0000
Subject: Consolidated QA kit onboarding plan + dataset retention windows

Hi Maya —

Thanks again for taking time yesterday. As discussed, we want to make the evaluation exchange as low-friction as possible while protecting Chronicle's IP and meeting your procurement needs. Attached is a first-pass QA_Kit_v1.zip that contains: a limited prompt pack (redacted customer PII), a 150-testcase seed set, and a small README with ingestion recommendations.

Key asks from our side:
- Confirm the narrow usage permissions (only evaluation against REDWOOD_PROD models for 60 days, no re-distribution)
- Confirm retention window for raw logs and derived embeddings (we propose 90 days for raw logs, 1 year for masked embeddings)
- Agree on a mutual action plan (MAP) with clear milestones so we can align engineering support windows

Proposed high-level MAP timeline (subject to your procurement):
- 7/21: QA kit delivery (this email)
- 7/23: Chronicle ingest validation (internal)
- 7/28: First eval run + quality review
- 8/04: Review call to agree on go/no-go and next steps

Please let us know if Chronicle needs alternate retention windows or an additional redaction pass. If procurement wants a different SOW shape I can loop legal immediately.

Thanks,
Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference
https://redwood.com

Attachment: QA_Kit_v1.zip (prompt_pack/seed_testcases/README.md)

From: Maya Gupta <maya@chronicle.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Arun Patel <arun@chronicle.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 23 Jul 2026 13:41:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Consolidated QA kit onboarding plan + dataset retention windows

Thanks Aisha — appreciate the quick turnaround and the clear timeline. A couple of things from my side after a quick pass:

- Usage: Chronicle is comfortable with a 60-day evaluation window, but we need a contractual clause that prohibits retention beyond agreed windows and forbids derivative dataset creation for third-party sharing.
- Redaction: The prompt_pack looks good but could you confirm whether any system-level logs will include unhashed user identifiers? We require either hashing or tokenization before storage.
- Procurement: Copying our procurement lead (procurement@chronicle.ai). They may propose edits to the SOW.

We'll review the SOW draft and get back with a procurement redline by end of this week. For technical items, can Rishi confirm the ingest and watermarking/tracing approach?

—Maya
Maya Gupta
Head of Product, Chronicle Labs
Attachment: SOW_Draft_Chronicle.pdf

On Tue, Jul 21, 2026 at 09:12 Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com> wrote:
> [original message omitted]

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Maya Gupta <maya@chronicle.ai>, Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 24 Jul 2026 15:06:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Consolidated QA kit onboarding plan + dataset retention windows

Hi Maya —

Quick technical notes to address your questions:
- Ingest: We can offer two ingest modes: (1) Redwood-managed staging (default) where we store raw logs for 90 days in an encrypted bucket with access audits, or (2) Chronicle-managed S3 ingestion into a private VPC where Redwood only receives masked/hashed artifacts.
- Identifiers: We will apply one-way hashing to any user identifier fields before they are written to persistent logs, by default. If Chronicle prefers tokenization with a custody key we can support that via your KMS.
- Watermarking/tracing: For testcases we can embed lightweight artifacts (SHA256 of testcase ID + salt) into our usage logs so we can prove lineage without exposing raw data. That allows rollback and deletion if requested.

Attachment: ingest_playbook.md (ingest steps, endpoints, and sample curl commands)

If procurement needs a stronger retention cap we can set the raw log TTL to 30 days; that will change observability for debugging but is doable.

Cheers,
Rishi Malhotra
Senior Reliability Engineer, Redwood Inference

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Maya Gupta <maya@chronicle.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Chronicle Procurement <procurement@chronicle.ai>
Date: Tue, 28 Jul 2026 10:20:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Consolidated QA kit onboarding plan + dataset retention windows — MAP + sign-offs

Thanks everyone — summarizing decisions and next actions below so we have a single source of truth. Please reply with any corrections.

Finalized points:
- Usage: Evaluation-only, 60-day active window on Redwood endpoints. No redistribution or derivative dataset publication.
- Retention: Raw logs TTL set to 30 days (Chronicle request). Masked embeddings retained up to 1 year.
- Identifiers: One-way hashing of user ids by default; Chronicle may opt for KMS tokenization (action: Chronicle to confirm).
- Lineage: We will include salted SHA256 artifacts to prove testcase lineage — non-reversible.

MAP milestones: (attached MAP_timeline_Chronicle.xlsx)
- 7/28: Chronicle ingest verification complete (Chronicle confirms pass/fail)
- 7/30: First eval run on Redwood dedicated infra (Redwood runs and shares quality report)
- 8/04: Review + go/no-go meeting (final exec decision)
- 8/11: If go, procurement SOW signature and prepare production pilot (reserved capacity planning)

Next actions:
- Chronicle procurement to send SOW redlines (procurement@chronicle.ai) — Arun please confirm timeline
- Chronicle to confirm whether they want Redwood-managed staging or VPC tokenization by 7/30
- Redwood (Rishi) to provide a short checklist for ingest validation by EOD 7/28
- Aisha to push final MAP and DocuSign link after procurement alignment

Thanks,
Aisha Rahman
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference

Attachment: MAP_timeline_Chronicle.xlsx
Link: https://app.docusign.com/embeddedSigning?token=abc123-chronicle-map

From: Arun Patel <arun@chronicle.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Maya Gupta <maya@chronicle.ai>
Cc: procurement@chronicle.ai, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Jul 2026 16:40:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Consolidated QA kit onboarding plan + dataset retention windows — procurement confirmation

Hi Aisha / Team —

Procurement here. We reviewed the SOW_Draft and the MAP timeline. Two quick notes:
1) We'll request a 30-day payment term and ask for a modest pilot credit if we sign by 8/11 (internal escalation in progress).
2) We confirm Chronicle prefers the VPC/tokenization option for sensitive testcases. Please update the SOW to reference KMS key rotation and audit log access for our security team.

We aim to send redlines by 7/30. Once redlines are in, please push the DocuSign envelope so we can loop legal.

Arun Patel
Procurement Manager, Chronicle Labs
procurement@chronicle.ai

preview:
From: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
To: Liam Turner <liam@stratagem.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>

preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-07-25T15:12:00-07:00
Subject: Exec liaison: decision path, procurement timeline, and open asks

Hi Ben / Monica (looping Lauren for visibility),

Thanks for joining the discovery with Sara and Tom at HeliosHealth earlier. Quick recap and proposed path so we have a single source of truth for exec/ procurement alignment: 

1) Top pain points they called out
- Inference cost and unpredictability when they move from pilot to clinician-facing volume (peak vs baseline).
- End-to-end latency impacting charting workflows (90th percentile spikes during peak hours).
- Compliance: SOC 2 evidence, data residency requirements for certain PHI-derived features.
- Fear of model drift and quality regressions -- they need rollback/fallback guarantees.

2) Business outcomes they want
- 30% reduction in inference unit cost vs current provider on 3M monthly tokens.
- Sub-200ms median inference latency in their primary region for clinical flows.
- Auditability and log retention for queries used in clinical decisions.

3) Proposed decision path (owner / ETA)
- Redwood to deliver MAP + two cost-band options (conservative / aggressive) — owner: Alex — due: 2026-07-27
- Legal procurement template + redline window estimate — owner: Monica — due: 2026-08-10
- PO target / commercial signoff — owner: Helios procurement (Tom) — target: 2026-08-20 (TBD)
- Pilot kickoff (Dedicated small pool) — owner: CS (TBD) — target: 2026-09-07

4) Open commercial asks from call
- Helios wants 3 cost scenarios (pilot, scaled, and burst) with per-token math and expected savings.
- They asked for a short exec one-pager showing business outcomes and SLA commitments for leadership review.
- Procurement asked for a one-week legal review window; they will provide contract template.

Attachments: helios_map.xlsx (mutual action plan stub), redwood_mup_agreement_v1.pdf (example MUP).

Immediate asks from our side:
- Ben: can you confirm the rough discount bands we can offer at $X commit (ballpark)?
- Monica: can you pre-skim the redline risks we should expect so we can advise Helios on timelines?
- Lauren/CS: identify an onboarding lead we can name in the MAP to shorten governance cycles.

I'll turn this into a short external-facing summary and send to Sara/Tom today with the MAP link.

— Alex Martinez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0192



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>

Subject: Re: Exec liaison: decision path, procurement timeline, and open asks

- I'll propose a high-level commercial band: ~20% discount at modest commit, ~35% at a two-quarter committed baseline (we'll firm numbers after we pull unit economics).

title: Packaging portability assessment and surge pilot proposal
preview:
From: Mira Singh <mira@aerosense.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Diego Ramos <diego.ramos@aerosense.ai>
Date: Sat, 25 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Packaging question — feature portability + surge behavior

Camila,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Quick follow-up from our product/ops review: we have two principal asks as we think about a tier move later this quarter.

1) Portability: If we upgrade from Compact to Throughput, can we keep our per-team entitlements and feature flags (reranking, structured-output) active without refactoring prompts or API routes? In particular, we need clarity on whether feature gating lives at the account level or the plan level.

2) Surge handling: During periodic batch jobs we spike for ~30–45 minutes. How does a temporary surge interact with reserved capacity vs. burst credits? Can we run a pilot where we keep our Compact seat count but get temporary throughput for a 30-day high-demand test?

I've attached a short usage export (aero_usage_summary_q2.csv) to give you a sense of token shape and burst cadence.

If useful, Diego (copied) can share more context on scheduling and procurement constraints — we have a narrow window for any change before their fiscal close.

Appreciate any packaging guidance/examples. Would love an outline of options + a recommended canary approach.

Best,
Mira Singh
Head of Product, AeroSense Analytics




From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Mira Singh <mira@aerosense.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 25 Jul 2026 12:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Packaging question — feature portability + surge behavior

Hi Mira — thanks for sending the usage file, that helps a lot. Short answers up front, then a recommended canary: 

- Portability: feature gating is account-scoped at Redwood; moving tiers does not force feature flag changes, but there are two caveats: 1) some entitlements (e.g., reserved KV cache size) are tied to the plan and will increase with Throughput; 2) if you change model variants as part of an upgrade (quantized vs. full fp16), there may be small prompt-engineering tweaks needed.

- Surge handling: we support a 2-branch approach: reserved capacity for baseline + burst credits for short spikes. For a pilot we can enable a temporary throughput overlay (30 days) that sits on top of your Compact seats — effectively a limited dedicated burst pool. That overlay can be instrumented and rolled back without changing your existing routes.

Recommended canary (what I'd do):
- 30-day pilot overlay (Throughput overlay) scoped to a single production route (reranking pipeline) — limits based on your Q2 spikes.
- Day 0–3: shadow traffic and metric collection (latency, cost per token, failure rate).
- Day 4–14: gradual cutover for 10–40% of reranking calls (canary ramp).
- Day 15–30: full route switch on overlay, with automatic fallback to Compact if latency or cost regressions exceed thresholds.

I've attached a packaging options spreadsheet that sketches two buy paths and a pilot structure (packaging_options_v2.xlsx). Ben — can you confirm pricing/concession options for a 30-day overlay pilot and what formal discount we could extend for a 12-month commit following a successful pilot? Lauren — we may need a short SOW amendment to document the overlay and rollback criteria.

Mira — if that plan looks reasonable I'll loop in Solutions Eng for a short scoping call next week. What days work for you?

Thanks,
Camila Reyes
Account Executive, Redwood Inference




From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Jul 2026 10:20:00 -0700

From: Mira Singh <mira@aerosense.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Diego Ramos <diego.ramos@aerosense.ai>
Date: Sat, 25 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Packaging question — feature portability + surge behavior

Camila,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Quick follow-up from our product/ops review: we have two principal asks as we think about a tier move later this quarter.

1) Portability: If we upgrade from Compact to Throughput, can we keep our per-team entitlements and feature flags (reranking, structured-output) active without refactoring prompts or API routes? In particular, we need clarity on whether feature gating lives at the account level or the plan level.

2) Surge handling: During periodic batch jobs we spike for ~30–45 minutes. How does a temporary surge interact with reserved capacity vs. burst credits? Can we run a pilot where we keep our Compact seat count but get temporary throughput for a 30-day high-demand test?

I've attached a short usage export (aero_usage_summary_q2.csv) to give you a sense of token shape and burst cadence.

If useful, Diego (copied) can share more context on scheduling and procurement constraints — we have a narrow window for any change before their fiscal close.

Appreciate any packaging guidance/examples. Would love an outline of options + a recommended canary approach.

Best,
Mira Singh
Head of Product, AeroSense Analytics




From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Mira Singh <mira@aerosense.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 25 Jul 2026 12:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Packaging question — feature portability + surge behavior

Hi Mira — thanks for sending the usage file, that helps a lot. Short answers up front, then a recommended canary: 

- Portability: feature gating is account-scoped at Redwood; moving tiers does not force feature flag changes, but there are two caveats: 1) some entitlements (e.g., reserved KV cache size) are tied to the plan and will increase with Throughput; 2) if you change model variants as part of an upgrade (quantized vs. full fp16), there may be small prompt-engineering tweaks needed.

- Surge handling: we support a 2-branch approach: reserved capacity for baseline + burst credits for short spikes. For a pilot we can enable a temporary throughput overlay (30 days) that sits on top of your Compact seats — effectively a limited dedicated burst pool. That overlay can be instrumented and rolled back without changing your existing routes.

Recommended canary (what I'd do):
- 30-day pilot overlay (Throughput overlay) scoped to a single production route (reranking pipeline) — limits based on your Q2 spikes.
- Day 0–3: shadow traffic and metric collection (latency, cost per token, failure rate).
- Day 4–14: gradual cutover for 10–40% of reranking calls (canary ramp).
- Day 15–30: full route switch on overlay, with automatic fallback to Compact if latency or cost regressions exceed thresholds.

I've attached a packaging options spreadsheet that sketches two buy paths and a pilot structure (packaging_options_v2.xlsx). Ben — can you confirm pricing/concession options for a 30-day overlay pilot and what formal discount we could extend for a 12-month commit following a successful pilot? Lauren — we may need a short SOW amendment to document the overlay and rollback criteria.

Mira — if that plan looks reasonable I'll loop in Solutions Eng for a short scoping call next week. What days work for you?

Thanks,
Camila Reyes
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Subject: Comp band specs + offer authorization: workstream and next steps

Team,

I pulled the latest benchmarking and baked it into a V3 comp band draft (attached). Key context: CompBench ran market medians across the AI infra / platform market (US West / East aggregated) and we added a set of geo anchors for EU/APAC projected next quarter. The goals for this thread:

- Agree on a clean definition for each level (IC L1-L6, Mgr L2-L4) we hand to recruiting.
- Finalize geo multipliers we use for offer guidance (anchoring vs market).
- Confirm offer approval gates (authorization levels for hiring managers, L4/L5 + GC signoff?).

Ask: Please review comp-band-draft-v3.xlsx and the geo-anchor reference PDF, comment inline in Drive by Friday EOD PDT. I’ve sketched suggested manager/PeopleOps approval thresholds in the sheet — these are intentionally conservative to start.

Immediate notes in the sheet:
- V3 moves Senior IC (L4) midpoint up 6% vs V2 based on Q2 comp inflation observed in San Francisco.
- We propose an explicit ‘above-market’ approval flow for any offer > 10% above midpoint (requires VP + People Lead signoff).

If nobody objects, I’ll route the doc to CompBench (external) for a quick reconciliation of their peer set and then send an internal summary to leadership next week.

Thanks — Ben

--
Ben Carter
Head of Talent Partners (Hiring Operations)
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

Attachments: comp-band-draft-v3.xlsx (spreadsheet), geo-anchor-reference.pdf (report)

Ben — thanks for pulling this together. Quick read-through notes: 

1) Level definitions: please separate out scope (impact/scope vs IC vs manager) into a short rubric column — makes calibration calls much faster.

2) Approval gates: the >10% threshold is fine but recommend we specify token budget authority for hiring managers vs directors (e.g., up to 3% above midpoint without HC lead). Otherwise we’ll get escalations for every Sr IC hire.

3) Geo multipliers: the PDF shows APAC as +8% to US mid; that feels high for SG and AU — ask CompBench to break APAC into SG/AU/IN.

I’ll join a sync if you want to run a 30-min calibration with Recruiting + two hiring managers on Thursday.

Thanks for sharing the draft. We ran the updated peer set across our US/EU/APAC buckets and attached a short reconciliation. Highlights we observed:

- APAC: SG and AU are indeed closer to US-equivalent ranges for senior technical ICs; India (Bengaluru/Hyderabad) is ~35-45% below US mid depending on role complexity.
- We recommend splitting APAC into: Australia / Singapore (anchor +2% to US mid) and India (anchor -38% to US mid).

We’ve uploaded a short PDF that maps suggested band midpoints and 25/75 percentiles for each market. If you want, we can prepare a one-page summary for Recruiting to use in offer training.
